title: Knip false-positive: url shim is not dead
description: knip flags src/shims/url.ts as unused but vite.config.ts maps the Node built-in 'url' module to this file via resolve.alias so all generated API files (common.ts and every *-api.ts) resolve correctly in the browser. The file is load-bearing.
recommendation: No action needed. Add a comment in the shim referencing vite.config.ts so future knip runs are easier to triage.

title: Codegen barrel — confirmed unused at runtime
description: src/api/generated/api.ts is a flat re-export barrel emitted by openapi-generator. The application imports directly from the granular api/* files (config.ts lines 21-32). The barrel is never imported.
recommendation: Safe to delete the generated barrel if the codegen template is updated to not emit it. Do not hand-delete; the next code-gen run will recreate it anyway. Document in the code-gen script.

title: Codegen index barrel — confirmed unused at runtime
description: src/api/generated/index.ts re-exports the barrel and model index. No non-generated file imports from it. Same cause as RC-002.
recommendation: Same recommendation as RC-002 — update code-gen template.

title: Codegen server-config API class — no frontend consumer
description: The ServerConfigApi class is generated but never imported outside generated code. The /api/v1/config endpoint is consumed via the handwritten ConfigController on the backend; the frontend reads config on boot via its own fetch. No frontend store or component imports this file.
recommendation: Info-only. If a future feature requires reading server config from the frontend this class is ready. Otherwise consider disabling server-config in the openapi-generator input spec.

title: DetailSidebar component unreachable — dead file
description: knip correctly flags DetailSidebar.tsx as unused. Searching all non-generated .tsx/.ts files confirms zero imports of DetailSidebar. The component was likely superseded during the namespace-detail redesign (commits 577f7d4 / 295d9b9).
recommendation: Delete the file. It carries no test coverage and is not part of any route or dynamic import chain.

title: updatesApi export has no non-test consumer
description: updatesApi is exported at line 100 of config.ts but appears only in test mocks (pluginStore.test.ts:32; TopBar.test.tsx:40; FilterBar.test.tsx:34; CatalogPage.test.tsx:39) as a mock shape and never imported for actual API calls. The updates feature appears unimplemented.
recommendation: Either remove the export and the UpdatesApi import if the updates feature is deferred post-beta.1; or add a tracking issue. Do not leave a dangling export pointing at a real API class.

title: @mui/icons-material is an unused dependency
description: knip confirms @mui/icons-material (line 25 of package.json) has no import in the frontend source tree. Icon needs are met by lucide-react (Globe; Upload; Activity; ExternalLink etc.). The package adds ~5 MB to node_modules.
recommendation: Remove from dependencies: pnpm remove @mui/icons-material

title: Re-export barrel in AdminSettingsPage is dead
description: Lines 23-26 re-export GeneralSection; UsersSection; OidcProvidersSection; ReviewsSection from the individual files. The router (src/router/index.tsx lines 43-60) uses lazy dynamic imports directly from the sub-files and never imports through AdminSettingsPage. These re-exports are the source of the knip 'unused export' findings for all four section components.
recommendation: Remove lines 23-26. The sections remain importable directly from their source files for any future consumer.

title: computeOffset is exported but never called
description: knip flags computeOffset (line 135 of timezones.ts) as an unused export. Grep across all non-generated non-test .ts/.tsx files confirms zero call sites. The function computes a UTC offset string for display purposes but TimezoneSelect and Timestamp both use the timezone identifier directly.
recommendation: Remove the export or make it unexported (drop the export keyword) until a consumer exists.

title: FormatOptions interface exported but never imported by name
description: knip flags the FormatOptions type as unused. All callers (PluginCard.tsx; PluginListRow.tsx; Timestamp.tsx) pass inline object literals to formatDateTime without importing FormatOptions. The interface is structurally satisfied but never named.
recommendation: Either remove the export keyword (keep as internal type) or suppress with a knip config entry if the public API intent is deliberate.

title: validate() body duplicated verbatim in UserSettingKey
description: SettingKey.validate() (lines 112-144) and UserSettingKey.validate() (lines 75-93) implement the same SettingValueType dispatch (STRING / INTEGER / BOOLEAN / ENUM) with near-identical code. Any change to validation rules (e.g. adding a new SettingValueType) must be applied in both files.
recommendation: Extract a top-level fun validateSettingValue(valueType: SettingValueType; rawValue: String; allowedValues: Set<String>?; minInt: Int?; maxInt: Int?): String? in a shared file (e.g. SettingValidation.kt) and delegate from both enums.

title: validateTimezoneOrEmpty duplicates validateTimezone logic from SettingKey.kt
description: SettingKey.kt defines private fun validateTimezone() (lines 29-34) and UserSettingKey.kt defines private fun validateTimezoneOrEmpty() (lines 26-34). Both wrap ZoneId.of() in a try-catch. The only difference is the empty-string fast-path in the user variant.
recommendation: Consolidate into a single internal fun validateIanaTimezone(rawValue: String; allowEmpty: Boolean = false): String? in a shared SettingValidation.kt.

title: Client IP extraction duplicated across LoginRateLimitFilter and CatalogController
description: LoginRateLimitFilter.resolveClientIp() (lines 79-85) and the inline snippet in CatalogController.downloadRelease() (lines 166-169) implement the same X-Forwarded-For / remoteAddr fallback pattern. The controller version is a one-liner and subtly different: it uses firstOrNull instead of first.
recommendation: Extract an extension fun HttpServletRequest.resolveClientIp(): String into a shared RequestUtils.kt. Both call sites use the same utility.

title: Visibility filter logic duplicated between findPagedByNamespace and findDistinctTags
description: PluginService.findPagedByNamespace() (lines 122-135) and findDistinctTags() (lines 282-294) each contain a when(visibility) block with identical PUBLIC / AUTHENTICATED / ADMIN predicate logic but the AUTHENTICATED branch in findDistinctTags omits the draft-only check. This divergence is a latent inconsistency.
recommendation: Extract a private fun visibilityPredicate(visibility: CatalogVisibility; pluginIdsWithPublishedRelease: Set<UUID>; draftOnlyPluginIds: Set<UUID>): (PluginEntity) -> Boolean and reuse it in both callers.

title: isSuperadmin overloaded twice with shared body — one overload is private
description: isSuperadmin(Authentication) at line 126 and isSuperadmin(String) at line 147 have identical repository calls. The public overload calls the private one for the username lookup but duplicates the key-prefix guard at the call site. The private overload exists only because requireRole passes a bare username string.
recommendation: Eliminate the private overload; requireRole should extract the name once and pass the Authentication object to the public method — or inline the guard at the one call site.

title: SecurityContextHolder.getContext().authentication!! used six times in one controller
description: ManagementController repeats SecurityContextHolder.getContext().authentication!! at lines 58; 64; 86; 109; 120; 130 — six times in 150 lines — including twice within a single method (updatePlugin lines 58 and 64). The !! non-null assert bypasses Kotlin null-safety and will throw NullPointerException if the security context is missing at runtime.
recommendation: Resolve the authentication once per method into a local val (or inject it as a @AuthenticationPrincipal parameter). The Kotlin coding style rule prohibits !!.

title: SecurityContextHolder.getContext().authentication!! repeated in ReviewsController
description: Same pattern as RC-016: ReviewsController uses !! dereference at lines 47; 68; 85.
recommendation: Same fix: resolve authentication once per method or use Spring's @AuthenticationPrincipal injection.

title: SecurityContextHolder.getContext().authentication!! repeated in NamespaceMemberController
description: NamespaceMemberController repeats the !! pattern at lines 79; 93; 127; 140.
recommendation: Same fix as RC-016 / RC-017.

title: defaultLanguage() accessor declared but never called outside the settings module
description: GeneralSettingsService.defaultLanguage() (line 110) and siteName() (line 113) are typed accessors defined symmetrically with the others but zero callers exist in the backend source tree (confirmed by grep). The frontend reads these values directly through the admin settings REST endpoint and the config endpoint only exposes defaultTimezone and maxFileSizeMb.
recommendation: Mark with a KDoc @see note or remove the accessor. If a future feature needs site-name or language server-side the accessor pattern is already in place; leave as-is only if that is planned.

title: GENERAL_DEFAULT_LANGUAGE setting key has only one allowed value and is structurally frozen
description: GENERAL_DEFAULT_LANGUAGE (line 61) declares allowedValues = setOf("en") with no path to adding other languages. The corresponding frontend dropdown (GeneralSection.tsx line 234) falls back to a hardcoded ["en"; "de"] when allowedValues is empty — meaning "de" would be selectable in the UI but the backend would reject it. The SettingKey enum entry and the GeneralSettingsService.defaultLanguage() accessor (RC-019) are both effectively inert until a real i18n feature lands.
recommendation: Document the incompleteness with a link to the i18n issue or collapse the key into a plain constant until the feature is real.

title: TODO without issue reference in SecurityConfiguration
description: Line 87 contains TODO(Phase 3): make frame-ancestors configurable for embeddable UI component. There is no linked issue number. This is the only TODO found across the entire backend source tree.
recommendation: Replace with a reference to the tracking issue (e.g. TODO #xxx) or remove the comment if the feature was deferred indefinitely.

title: findPagedByNamespace loads all plugins then filters in-memory — inline comment acknowledges it
description: The method loads all plugins for a namespace via pluginRepository.findAllByNamespace (line 99) and applies tag; full-text; visibility; version; and sort filters in-memory across potentially hundreds of entities. The comment at line 97 says "acceptable for MVP catalog sizes". Additionally findLatestPublishedReleasesForPlugins is called twice (lines 108 and 155) when a version filter is active — one call per page render.
recommendation: This is a performance smell masked as a simplicity trade-off. The method is already 110 lines. Flag for extraction to a query-based approach before the catalog grows beyond ~500 plugins. Not blocking for beta.1 but should be a tracked issue.

title: compareVersions private helper duplicates semver comparison available in SemVerExtensions
description: PluginService contains a private compareVersions(a: String; b: String): Int (lines 205-215) and matchesVersionConstraint (lines 199-203). The spi module already ships plugwerk-spi/src/main/kotlin/io/plugwerk/spi/version/SemVerExtensions.kt with semantic version utilities. The inline implementation in PluginService is simpler but diverges from the canonical logic.
recommendation: Use or extend SemVerExtensions rather than maintaining a second implementation.

title: validateLocally() in GeneralSection reimplements the same type-dispatch logic as the backend SettingKey.validate()
description: GeneralSection.tsx lines 119-155 implement a validateLocally() function with the same STRING / INTEGER / BOOLEAN / ENUM dispatch that SettingKey.kt already performs server-side. This is expected (client-side feedback) but the INTEGER bounds check (lines 126-137) is more lenient than the server — it does not reject leading zeros or very large floats that parseInt would accept via parseFloat path. Any new backend type must be reflected here manually.
recommendation: Low priority for beta.1. Document that validateLocally must stay in sync with SettingKey.validate() in a code comment; or derive the validation rules from the DTO (minInt; maxInt; valueType) already returned by the API.

title: SecurityContextHolder.getContext().authentication!! repeated in AccessKeyController
description: AccessKeyController uses the !! dereference at lines 46; 59; 81 (confirmed by grep).
recommendation: Same fix as RC-016: resolve once per method or use @AuthenticationPrincipal.
